2026-04-22T05:21:59.7329146Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-22T05:21:59.7329687Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-22T05:21:59.7330107Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-22T05:21:59.7330470Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-22T05:21:59.7330766Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-22T05:21:59.7330998Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-22T05:21:59.7331154Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-22T05:21:59.7331332Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-22T05:21:59.7331498Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-22T05:21:59.7331720Z [36;1m{[0m

2026-04-22T05:21:59.7332206Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-22T05:21:59.7332482Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

Use this when work is complete, the issue is no longer relevant, or it's a duplicate. The closing comment should explain the resolution or reason for closing. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be closed. Target: *.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"issue_number":{"description":"Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"state_reason":{"description":"The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.","enum":["COMPLETED","NOT_PLANNED","DUPLICATE"],"type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"},"name":"close_issue"},{"description":"Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *. Supports reply_to_id for discussion threading.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"reply_to_id":{"description":"Node ID of the discussion comment to reply to, enabling threaded discussion comments. When provided, the new comment is posted as a reply to the specified top-level discussion comment. If the given node ID belongs to a nested reply, the handler automatically resolves it to the top-level parent. Only applicable for discussion comments — ignored for issue and pull request comments.","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.","pattern":"^aw_[A-Za-z0-9]{3,12}$","type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_comment"},{"description":"Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. Target: *.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"Issue or PR number to add labels to. If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.","type":"number"},"labels":{"description":"Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["labels"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_labels"},{"description":"Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. Target: *.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"Issue or PR number to remove labels from. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.","type":"number"},"labels":{"description":"Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.","items":{"type":"string"},"minItems":1,"type":"array"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["labels"],"type":"object"},"name":"remove_labels"},{"description":"Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"assignee":{"description":"Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.","type":"string"},"assignees":{"description":"GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"issue_number":{"description":"Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["issue_number"],"type":"object"},"name":"assign_to_user"},{"description":"Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Version `1.0.0` was stamped on 2026-04-21.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **Key concepts section is empty/generic** — only links to the generic Azure SDK introduction page. It should describe CustomerInsights-specific concepts: `HubResource`, Profiles, Interactions, Connectors, Relationships, Views, KPIs, and Authorization Policies.\\n- **No inline code examples** — the \\u0026#34;Examples\\u0026#34; section only points to the generic `aka.ms/azuresdk-net-mgmt-samples` link without any service-specific snippets. At minimum, creating a Hub and listing its profiles should be demonstrated.\\n- **No Samples test files** — the `tests/` directory has no `Samples/` subfolder with snippet-annotated NUnit test methods. These are required to generate README snippets via `Update-Snippets.ps1`.\\n- **README header and \\u0026#34;Getting Started\\u0026#34; still use beta-era generic boilerplate** — for a GA release the description should be updated to drop references to \\u0026#34;Public Preview\\u0026#34; and use definitive language about capabilities.\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### Edit `sdk/customer-insights/Azure.ResourceManager.CustomerInsights/README.md`\\n\\nReplace the `## Key concepts` section (currently a single generic link) with a service-specific section:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\n### Hub\\n\\nA **Hub** (`HubResource`) is the top-level Customer Insights resource that lives in an Azure Resource Group. All other Customer Insights resources (Profiles, Interactions, Connectors, etc.) are scoped under a Hub.\\n\\n### Profiles and Interactions\\n\\n**Profiles** represent the customer entities (e.g., a person or account). **Interactions** represent customer activities (e.g., a purchase or a support call). Both are defined as schema definitions inside a Hub.\\n\\n### Connectors and Connector Mappings\\n\\n**Connectors** define the data source (e.g., Azure Blob Storage) used to ingest data. **Connector Mappings** describe how incoming data maps to Profiles or Interactions.\\n\\n### Views, KPIs, and Relationships\\n\\nThese sub-resources let you surface aggregated metrics, define data relationships across Profiles, and create custom views over the data.\\n```\\n\\nReplace the `## Examples` section with a placeholder that will be filled in by the snippet tool:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Examples\\n\\n### Create a Hub\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### List Hubs in a subscription\\n\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n\\n#### Create `sdk/customer-insights/Azure.ResourceManager.CustomerInsights/tests/Samples/Sample1_HelloWorld.cs`\\n\\nCreate the file with the following content:\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\\n\\nusing System;\\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\\nusing Azure.Identity;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.CustomerInsights;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.CustomerInsights.Models;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.Resources;\\nusing NUnit.Framework;\\n\\nnamespace Azure.ResourceManager.CustomerInsights.Tests.Samples\\n{\\n    public class Sample1_HelloWorld : SamplesBase\\n    {\\n        [Test]\\n        [SyncOnly]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample compiles\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task CreateHub()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:CreateHub\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await subscription.GetResourceGroups().GetAsync(\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n            HubCollection hubs = resourceGroup.GetHubs();\\n            HubData hubData = new HubData(AzureLocation.WestUS);\\n            ArmOperation lro = await hubs.CreateOrUpdateAsync(WaitUntil.Completed, \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;, hubData);\\n            HubResource hub = lro.Value;\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Hub created: {hub.Data.Name}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [SyncOnly]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample compiles\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task ListHubs()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:ListHubs\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await subscription.GetResourceGroups().GetAsync(\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n            await foreach (HubResource hub in resourceGroup.GetHubs())\\n            {\\n                Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Hub: {hub.Data.Name}, Location: {hub.Data.Location}\\u0026#34;);\\n            }\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update Version `1.0.0` was stamped on 2026-04-21.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **Key concepts section is empty/generic** — only links to the generic Azure SDK introduction page. It should describe CustomerInsights-specific concepts: `HubResource`, Profiles, Interactions, Connectors, Relationships, Views, KPIs, and Authorization Policies.\\n- **No inline code examples** — the \\u0026#34;Examples\\u0026#34; section only points to the generic `aka.ms/azuresdk-net-mgmt-samples` link without any service-specific snippets. At minimum, creating a Hub and listing its profiles should be demonstrated.\\n- **No Samples test files** — the `tests/` directory has no `Samples/` subfolder with snippet-annotated NUnit test methods. These are required to generate README snippets via `Update-Snippets.ps1`.\\n- **README header and \\u0026#34;Getting Started\\u0026#34; still use beta-era generic boilerplate** — for a GA release the description should be updated to drop references to \\u0026#34;Public Preview\\u0026#34; and use definitive language about capabilities.\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### Edit `sdk/customer-insights/Azure.ResourceManager.CustomerInsights/README.md`\\n\\nReplace the `## Key concepts` section (currently a single generic link) with a service-specific section:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\n### Hub\\n\\nA **Hub** (`HubResource`) is the top-level Customer Insights resource that lives in an Azure Resource Group. All other Customer Insights resources (Profiles, Interactions, Connectors, etc.) are scoped under a Hub.\\n\\n### Profiles and Interactions\\n\\n**Profiles** represent the customer entities (e.g., a person or account). **Interactions** represent customer activities (e.g., a purchase or a support call). Both are defined as schema definitions inside a Hub.\\n\\n### Connectors and Connector Mappings\\n\\n**Connectors** define the data source (e.g., Azure Blob Storage) used to ingest data. **Connector Mappings** describe how incoming data maps to Profiles or Interactions.\\n\\n### Views, KPIs, and Relationships\\n\\nThese sub-resources let you surface aggregated metrics, define data relationships across Profiles, and create custom views over the data.\\n```\\n\\nReplace the `## Examples` section with a placeholder that will be filled in by the snippet tool:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Examples\\n\\n### Create a Hub\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### List Hubs in a subscription\\n\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n\\n#### Create `sdk/customer-insights/Azure.ResourceManager.CustomerInsights/tests/Samples/Sample1_HelloWorld.cs`\\n\\nCreate the file with the following content:\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\\n\\nusing System;\\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\\nusing Azure.Identity;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.CustomerInsights;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.CustomerInsights.Models;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.Resources;\\nusing NUnit.Framework;\\n\\nnamespace Azure.ResourceManager.CustomerInsights.Tests.Samples\\n{\\n    public class Sample1_HelloWorld : SamplesBase\\n    {\\n        [Test]\\n        [SyncOnly]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample compiles\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task CreateHub()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:CreateHub\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await subscription.GetResourceGroups().GetAsync(\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n            HubCollection hubs = resourceGroup.GetHubs();\\n            HubData hubData = new HubData(AzureLocation.WestUS);\\n            ArmOperation lro = await hubs.CreateOrUpdateAsync(WaitUntil.Completed, \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;, hubData);\\n            HubResource hub = lro.Value;\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Hub created: {hub.Data.Name}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [SyncOnly]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample compiles\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task ListHubs()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:ListHubs\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await subscription.GetResourceGroups().GetAsync(\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n            await foreach (HubResource hub in resourceGroup.GetHubs())\\n            {\\n                Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Hub: {hub.Data.Name}, Location: {hub.Data.Location}\\u0026#34;);\\n            }\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nThe snippets are defined in the test file above using `#region Snippet:` / `#endregion`.\\n\\n**Snippet 1: `CreateHub`**\\n- Test file:
